heading: Identification of pests; cooperation with Department of Agriculture’s program
label: 136w–3 Identification of pests; cooperation with Department of Agriculture’s program
notes: Amendments1996—Subsec. (b)(2)(A). struck out “and the information required by ” after “paragraph (1)”.Pub. L. 104–127section 5882 of this titleSubsec. (d). added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 104–1701990— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a) and added subsecs. (b) and (c).Pub. L. 101–624
amendments: Amendments1996—Subsec. (b)(2)(A). struck out “and the information required by ” after “paragraph (1)”.Pub. L. 104–127section 5882 of this titleSubsec. (d). added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 104–1701990— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a) and added subsecs. (b) and (c).Pub. L. 101–624

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Administrator, in coordination with the Secretary of Agriculture, shall identify those pests that must be brought under control. The Administrator shall also coordinate and cooperate with the Secretary of Agriculture’s research and implementation programs to develop and improve the safe use and effectiveness of chemical, biological, and alternative methods to combat and control pests that reduce the quality and economical production and distribution of agricultural products to domestic and foreign consumers.

children.label: A
children.text: available methods of pest control by crop or animal;

children.label: B
children.text: minor pest control problems, both in minor crops and minor or localized problems in major crops; and

children.label: C
children.text: factors limiting the availability of specific pest control methods, such as resistance to control methods and regulatory actions limiting the availability of control methods.

children.label: A
children.text: contain the information described in paragraph (1);

children.label: B
children.text: identify the crucial pest control needs where a shortage of control methods is indicated by the information described in paragraph (1); and

children.label: C
children.text: describe in detail research and extension efforts designed to address the needs identified in subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Integrated pest management
children.label: c Integrated pest management
children.text: The Administrator, in cooperation with the Secretary of Agriculture, shall develop approaches to the control of pests based on integrated pest management that respond to the needs of producers, with a special emphasis on minor pests.

children.heading: Public health pests
children.label: d Public health pests
children.text: The Administrator, in coordination with the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of Health and Human Services, shall identify pests of significant public health importance and, in coordination with the Public Health Service, develop and implement programs to improve and facilitate the safe and necessary use of chemical, biological, and other methods to combat and control such pests of public health importance.
